steps.title: Extract the x-coordinate from the first equation
steps.content: The first equation $x = 49$ directly specifies the x-coordinate of the intersection point.

steps.title: Substitute x = 49 into the second equation to find y
steps.content:
With $x = 49$, substitute into $y = \sqrt{x} + 9$:
$$y = \sqrt{49} + 9 = 7 + 9 = 16$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
